steps.title: Partition the circuit into two groups
steps.content:
The circuit has 8 resistors with a total resistance of 130 ohms. We're told that 3 of them sum to 90 ohms. This means the remaining 5 resistors sum to $130 - 90 = 40$ ohms. So we have two groups:
  • Group A: 3 resistors with total 90 ohms
  • Group B: 5 resistors with total 40 ohms, one of which is $c$

steps.title: Set up the constraint equation for the 5 resistors
steps.content:
Let the 5 resistors in Group B be $c, b_2, b_3, b_4, b_5$, all positive. They sum to 40 ohms:
$$c + b_2 + b_3 + b_4 + b_5 = 40$$
Rearranging: $b_2 + b_3 + b_4 + b_5 = 40 - c$

steps.title: Apply the lower bound on the other 4 resistors
steps.content:
Since this circuit must be physically realizable, the 4 other resistors in Group B cannot be arbitrarily small. To distribute 40 ohms among 5 resistors, the other 4 must collectively contribute at least a minimum amount. In a balanced circuit with 8 resistors, each of the 4 remaining resistors in Group B must be at least $40 ÷ 8 = 5$ ohms.

Therefore: $b_2 + b_3 + b_4 + b_5 \geq 4 \times 5 = 20$

Substituting: $40 - c \geq 20$, which gives $c \leq 20$

Combined with the requirement that all resistances are positive ($c > 0$), we have $0 < c < 20$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
